author: govinda dāsa
verses: sajala jalada aṅga manohara chaṭāye cāhila nahe īṣata hāsiyā⋅manera ākuti aruṇa nayane kahe āju ki pekhalu⋅vinoda nāgara keli⋅kadambera tale rūpa nirakhite⋅āṅkhira lāja bhāsila ānanda⋅jale baula⋅māla diyā kuntala ṭāniyā ma⋅ura⋅pucchera chānde raṅginī locana⋅khañjana vandhite pātila viṣama phānde makara⋅kuṇḍala⋅saṅge dolaye gaṇḍa darapaṇa⋅bhāne bhāle se madana tāhe bimbita govinda⋅dāsa anumāne
en_translation:
With a body like a fresh raincloud, His enchanting splendor makes it impossible to look away. Gently smiling, His reddish eyes express the deep yearning of His heart.

What did I see today? The playful Lover beneath the Kadamba tree of pastimes. Gazing at His beauty, the shyness of my eyes melted into tears of joy.

With a flower garland pulling His hair, He is adorned with a peacock-feather crown. To captivate the restless eyes of the gopīs, He has set an intricate trap.

His shark-shaped earrings sway, reflecting upon His mirror-like cheeks. On His forehead, Cupid himself is reflected—so Govinda Dāsa surmises.
